realizing it. This dissociation continued thru all the eleven drafts + readings, until the memories, personality + knowledge - i.e. the other person broke into consciousness the month the book was released.

Question: Is there a literature on alternate personalities (in persons with multiple personalities) separated by 1900 years + 8000 miles? They could not have split off during one lifetime (i.e. at an early age). So childhood trauma won't explain it. Answer: There is no such literature.

Question: Does the stegenographic message provide any clue to the abreative personality? Answer: the message is the "Kerygma" (Proclaiming the redemptive death + resurrection of the Lord) revised. Christ is not crucified, did not depart + hence is present. The subjective sensations noted about pointed to a confirmation of this.

Conclusion: the person has a rich fantasy world. He has dissociated personalities + experiences (ever since an auto accident he has complained of periodic amnesia). The "hidden message" in the novel + the "remembering ancient Rome" indicate strong unconscious wishes directed toward Christ's imagines return.

It is touching that the human hopes for a redeemer could yield such elaborate phantasies. A dearth of real experiences is indicated. Therapy indicated. #

Zebra is an ETI secretly here, breaking this androidproducing deterministic coertion which we're happily oblivious to.

I see in the crucifixion story the message "punishment must end." In my schema, revealed to be, punishment is one of the key words, tied to the key word sin. This whole sin - punishment system is a [smoke]- stream for our enslavement. The solution to the equation sin-punishment➀ is innocence-joy, ➁ this is what the secret Kerygma in "Tears" reveals, + "Scanner" goes on to study the issue further. ➀ work ➁ play.

Then, by my theory, the last thing we should do is imitate Christ's passion. It was to liberate us from this that he came: to relieve us of the belief that suffering is natural + somehow proves or is tied to "sin."

As Lord of the universe it is his desire + mission to extricate us from -+ finally destroy - the Tears world. + a lof of the Tears world is psychological - i.e. a spiritual matter.

79
(78)

I need no proof of the Kerygma encoded in "Tears" - I got that in 2-74/3-74. We are not able to identify Zebra (Christ/Dionysos).

My guess that that which I saw + called Zebra had not (just as I guessed by the total lack of previous reports of such an experience as mine in 2000 years) been previously here. But had returned - otherwise, why the message in "Tears"? You do - well, it's less likely you'd smuggle in an encoded message about a static long term stable condition than re an event (a current one).

The message in "Tears" was news that something had happened, + that happening was his return. Amazed at what I saw I named it Zebra, not knowing what or who it was. I presume he now (still) invisibly works behind the scenes of world history as it unfolds, but the true hidden Xtian church knows. I, ahem, helped tell them.

"+ gained from heaven a friend." I feel that God takes note + is interested. That, most of all, he understands (what other people can't + so is sympathetic (" he sees even the fallen sparrow").

In a sense I would even define God that way" "as the sentience in the cosmos which understand."

But all this is not a way of saying man is a sinner (deficient in merits). What it says is, God's Love is for the desperate + the damned, not for the goody-goody, who you see all righteous. All the piety can't make it - God reaches down into the gutter, the people like in "Scanner." In a sense the Kerygma is: a suspension of punishment (+ a restoration of innocence). (The gift of innocence. Love + assistance + rescue is not a judicial matter.

In "Tears" the serious old King's verdict (Justice) is death. But Buckman turns grief over death into love; so love triumphs - not over death - but over the judicial verdict of death - + Taverner goes free. Within this sequence, the culmination of the book, the essential miracle of the Not is disclosed: the holy mystery + victory of love issuing out of greif + turning a sentence of guilty into innocence.

I simply do not see this as judicial. I see it as - a freeing man of the curse, a releasing him from his enslaved state. The court sat, the books were opened, +

In a way, the problem is, we can't figure out God's basis of selection. Code ethics did not provide an index. Whim probably is not the answer; some plan, purpose or pattern is involved, but we're too dumb to discern it.

It goes back to my concept of him posing us a problem to solve. Naturally we're not to develop a solution formula - that would defeat the purpose.

In a nutshell, my book announces the ushering in of the age of man's (restored) innocence, which is to say, Christ Returned the final Kerygma.

----------

Voice:"What my book (secretly) teaches is what w3e must teach in order to be Xtians." Wow.

86
(85)

When you consider the clear + precise way that the authentic Xtian Kerygma is presented in "Tears" - then my 3-74 experience becomes only one part, a completing part, of the Miracle. I received the gift of innocence, the suspension of punishment, awareness of the presence of Christ - i.e. proof of the truth of the presence of Christ - i.e. proof of the truth of the proclamation buried in the actual dramatic context (not just encoded textually) of the novel. The serious white haired old King is perhaps YHWH, overruled by the new covanent of love issuing out of the age of grief (i.e. Tears) into the age of joy + innocence.

The 3-74 experience without the encoded Kerygma in "Tears" is the full miracle. The encoded Kerygma is a full miracle. One is a verbal dramatizations + proclamation (analog) of the other. The two together are like the pleroma, Forms I + II combined (message + the reality that message describes).

I wonder when I knew that book was true, it always was so important to me. I feared so much for its safety.
